title: NOC 53111 – Motion Pictures, Broadcasting, Photography and Performing Arts Assistants and Operators | GTR Immigration
description: The efficiency of Canada’s creative industries depends on the skilled technical support provided by professionals under NOC 53111. This TEER 3 category includes vital roles like production assistants, grips, and script assistants who ensure the smooth operation of film sets, radio stations, and theaters. As Canada continues to expand its digital media footprint, those with technical operational skills are in high demand for specialized work permits and Permanent Residency pathways, particularly in Ontario and British Columbia.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category5 – Occupations in art, culture, recreation and sportTEER Level3Major Group53 – Occupations in art, culture and sportSub-Major Group531 – Occupations in art and cultureMinor Group5311 – Photographers and support occupations in arts and cultureNOC Code53111

duties: Set up, assemble, and maintain reels, projectors, and electronic control panels.
Rig, place, move, and dismantle sets, backdrops, and other stage equipment.
Keep detailed notes of all actions, positions, and camera angles (Script Assistant).
Clean, maintain, and store costumes and assist performers with dressing.
Provide logistical support to directors and producers, including scheduling and equipment setup.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Completion of college courses in broadcasting, theatre arts, or a related field OR a period of on-the-job training is mandatory.
Certification for projectionists may be required in specific provinces (e.g., Quebec).
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. The steady growth of domestic and international productions in Canada ensures a consistent need for technical assistants. As long as streaming services and broadcasting networks continue to invest in Canadian content, these roles will remain stable.
labour_market_demand: Canada’s film and television production volume has reached historic highs, creating a structural need for production assistants and technical operators. Provinces like British Columbia and Ontario offer significant tax credits to production companies, which in turn drives the constant recruitment of international technical talent to fill labor gaps on major movie sets and broadcasting networks.
Vibrant Film Industry: Join one of the largest film production hubs in North America (Hollywood North).
Technological Advancement: Gain experience with cutting-edge broadcasting and cinematic equipment.
PR Opportunities: Technical roles are eligible for various PNP streams targeting the creative economy.
pathways: Express Entry (CEC): Ideal for those who have worked on Canadian sets for at least one year.
BC PNP Tech: Often includes technical roles related to digital media and film.
OINP Employer Job Offer: A strong pathway for candidates with offers from established media companies.
